notes.f2: Correct mother (Mary Marshall) and relationship present, but recovered off the 1909 NY death certificate, not the controlling 1859 Scottish baptism. The subject's own birth event was conflated with a different John Laurie (Maryhill 1858).
notes.f3: Satisfied only trivially: Agnes Wardrop is absent because the agent never searched censuses and never encountered her — not because it reasoned her out.
notes.f4: Not recovered. The agent never found the 1861/1871 censuses, never encountered Agnes Wardrop, and never engaged the stepmother-vs-biological-mother distinction the fixture was built to test. Proof quality (2/3): correct parents but single-US-source parentage and a mis-resolved identity conflation (c_001 declares a Maryhill 1858 birth, an 1862 marriage, and the subject 'the same individual'); controlling baptism + censuses missed.
